name: Etangs de Bassiès - hike starting from Massada car park - Pyrénées Ariégeoises Regional Natural Park
name_original: Etangs de Bassiès – randonnée au départ du parking de Massada – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
region_intro: Hiking around Auzat offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Pyrénées Ariégeoises Regional Natural Park. The terrain is characterized by majestic mountain peaks, including the Montcalm Massif, and numerous picturesque lakes. The Vicdessos Valley, where Auzat is situated, features a blend of rivers, dense forests, and significant elevation changes. This region provides a variety of hiking trails across its mountainous environment. Best hiking trails around Auzat The most popular hiking route is Etangs de Bassiès - hike starting from Massada car park - Pyrénées Ariégeoises Regional Natural Park, a 7.9 miles (12.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route leads to a series of alpine lakes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Étang d'Izourt Loop via GR 10, a moderate 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. This trail offers views of Étang d'Izourt and follows a section of the GR10 long-distance route. Local hikers also love the Montcalm and Pica d'Estats Loop Trail, an 11.3 miles (18.1 km) trail leading through high mountain terrain, often completed in about 9 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Auzat is defined by prominent mountain peaks, serene alpine lakes, and dense forests within the Vicdessos Valley. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging high-altitude ascents. The routes in Auzat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 6000 hikers have used komoot to explore Auzat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Auzat?
A: There are over 70 hiking trails listed on komoot around Auzat, offering a wide range of options. The broader Pyrénées Ariégeoises Regional Natural Park, where Auzat is located, boasts over 550 kilometers of marked trails, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking trails in Auzat?
A: Auzat offers a diverse range of hiking difficulties. You'll find &lt;strong&gt;6 easy trails&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;31 moderate trails&lt;/strong&gt;, and &lt;strong&gt;39 difficult trails&lt;/strong&gt;. This variety ensures options for casual walkers and experienced mountaineers alike, with routes leading to high mountain peaks and serene alpine lakes.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see while hiking in Auzat?
A: The Auzat region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to encounter majestic mountain peaks like the Montcalm Massif, which includes the Pic du Montcalm and Pica d'Estats. Numerous picturesque lakes, or &lt;em&gt;étangs&lt;/em&gt;, such as the Étangs de Bassiès, Étang d'Izourt, and Étangs de la Gardelle, are popular destinations. The Vicdessos Valley also features stunning vistas, rivers, dense forests, and the beautiful Artigue waterfalls.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Auzat area?
A: Yes, Auzat offers several rewarding circular routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949892098/etang-d-izourt-loop-via-gr-10" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang d'Izourt Loop via GR 10&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.8 km path that provides views of the lake and follows a section of the famous GR10. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949298665/montcalm-and-pica-d-estats-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcalm and Pica d'Estats Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an 18.1 km route through high mountain terrain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Auzat?
A: The hiking trails around Auzat are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 540 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to tranquil lake trails, and the well-preserved natural environment of the Pyrénées Ariégeoises Regional Natural Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Auzat?
A: While many trails in Auzat involve significant elevation changes, there are options suitable for families. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' or 'moderate' on komoot. The region also offers other family-friendly activities like a tree-climbing park and a general sports park, which can complement shorter walks.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Auzat?
A: Many trails in the Pyrénées Ariégeoises Regional Natural Park are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to check specific trail regulations, especially in protected areas or near grazing livestock. Keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: What is the best season to go hiking in Auzat?
A: The best season for hiking in Auzat is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally milder, and trails are more accessible. High-altitude routes, especially those leading to peaks like Montcalm, are best attempted in summer (July-August) when snow has melted and conditions are most stable.

Q: Are there any huts or refuges along the hiking routes in Auzat?
A: Yes, the Auzat area, being part of a major hiking region, has several huts and refuges. Notable examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2829944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinet Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3833394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comapedrosa Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refuge Borda de Sorteny&lt;/a&gt;. These can provide essential shelter and sometimes food for longer treks.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near Auzat's hiking trails?
A: The Auzat region is dotted with numerous picturesque lakes (&lt;em&gt;étangs&lt;/em&gt;) such as Étang d'Izourt, Étangs de la Gardelle, and Étang de Soulcem. While not all are suitable or permitted for swimming, many offer refreshing spots for a dip after a hike, especially during warmer months. Always check local regulations and be mindful of water temperatures and safety.

Q: What are some notable attractions or points of interest near Auzat that I can visit?
A: Beyond hiking, Auzat offers several cultural and historical points of interest. You can explore the ruins of the 13th-century Château de Montréal-de-Sos or visit the Maison des Patrimoines, an interpretive center. For those interested in prehistory, the renowned Niaux Cave and the Prehistoric Park are also accessible nearby. Additionally, the Mirador Solar de Tristaina in Andorra offers breathtaking panoramic views.
tips: an approach that is not easy but well rewarded
The ponds of Bassiès are a set of ponds located north of the Pic Rouge de Bassiès. A guarded refuge is located west of the ponds, at the foot of the Cabanatous peak.
